title: Ponytail
voiceScript: What if your AI agent shipped 54% less code, and still passed every safety check? My agent kept over-building. Full React components for problems a native input solved. That is extra QA, extra edge cases, extra midnight fixes. So I installed Ponytail, a Claude Code skill. It runs a ladder before writing anything: delete, native, reuse, minimal. The result: 54% less code on average, sometimes up to 94%. Sessions run 27% faster, 20% cheaper. Discipline is subtraction, not motivation. Small PRs that review in minutes, not hours. If your agent talks itself into complexity, this ladder fixes it. Follow for more.

scenes.voiceText: What if your AI agent shipped 54% less code, and still passed every safety check?
scenes.headline: Ship *54%* less code

scenes.headline: Real benchmark gains

scenes.headline: Discipline is *subtraction*

scenes.voiceText: If your agent talks itself into complexity, this ladder fixes it. Follow for more.

source: LinkedIn post about Ponytail, a minimalist instruction set / plugin that makes AI coding agents think like a 'lazy senior developer.' Core philosophy: 'the best code is the code you never wrote.' Forces a ladder before writing any code: Delete (can this be solved by removing code), Native (does a built-in platform feature already do this, e.g. native <input>), Reuse (is there an existing helper/component), Minimal (smallest new code if code must be written). Stays strict on safety, security, accessibility, and validation, so minimal never means reckless. Benchmarks (Claude Code editing a FastAPI + React repo): avg 54% less code, up to 94% in over-building cases; sessions ~20% cheaper and ~27% faster from reduced token usage; 100% safety record in benchmarks vs. other terse prompts that drop safety guards. Commands: /ponytail [lite|full|ultra|off], /ponytail-review, /ponytail-audit, /ponytail-debt, /ponytail-gain. Plugin-based on Claude Code, Codex, GitHub Copilot CLI, Devin CLI, Gemini/Antigravity CLI; instruction-based via .cursorrules/.clinerules on Cursor, Windsurf, Cline, Aider; zero-setup auto-detect via AGENTS.md on CodeWhale/Swival. Post's own closing line: discipline is subtraction, not motivation.
